summary: Task 3 files (TapCounter, LogcatReader, DeviceInfoCollector, LogExportManager) introduce no security findings. A03 (argv injection): LogcatReader builds a six-element List<String> of fixed literals passed to ProcessBuilder(argv).redirectErrorStream(true).start() — no shell wrapper, no Runtime.exec(String), no String.split. The sole variable slot --pid=${Process.myPid()} interpolates an Int, which cannot emit shell metacharacters or whitespace; the argvShape_exactlySixArgs_knownPositions test locks both the element count and positions, and guardrails argv[4] with regex ^--pid=\d+$. A01/privacy: LogExportManager.writeTo uses the SAF content:// URI only to open an OutputStream; grep of the entire logexport package shows zero Log.* calls, so neither the URI, its path, nor the export payload is echoed to logcat or any secondary sink. All filesystem reads are rooted at context.filesDir with compile-time-constant filenames (crash.log, errors.log, errors.log.1) — zero user-controlled path components, no path traversal surface. A05: openOutputStreamForTest is `internal var`, enforced module-private by Kotlin/JVM; it is not exposed via Hilt @Provides/@Inject and is not referenced by :crash or :core-* — misuse would require compiling against :app internals, infeasible from a separate APK. The default lambda delegates to context.contentResolver.openOutputStream, matching production behavior. A09: writeTo does not catch IOException — it surfaces to the caller (verified by writeTo_ioException_surfaces and writeTo_nullOutputStream_surfacesAsIoException). The DefaultCommandRunner IOException/SecurityException swallows are deliberate per Decision 8 adjacency (paranoid-mode OEMs without logcat in PATH) and preserve the failure signal in-band as `[logcat unavailable: empty]`. A08: no deserialization (no JSON.parse of untrusted data, no ObjectInputStream, no YAML, no reflection on input). No hardcoded secrets; no network I/O; no dynamic class loading; no exported components introduced; String.format uses Locale.ROOT in formatGb (prevents locale drift in the .txt header). TapCounter is pure-JVM with no side channels. Decisions 1, 5, 7, 8, 10, 11 honored at the file level; Decision 11's accepted non-redaction tradeoff is respected — the export manager faithfully concatenates sources without adding new disclosure surfaces.
